author: y4rr
content:
Landed all 8 ops.

## What landed in \`ferrotorch-core/src/linalg.rs\`

### Public API
- \`solve_triangular(a, b, upper, transpose, unit_diagonal)\` — forward / back substitution. Supports 1-D and 2-D \`b\`, transpose flag (folded into effective triangle), unit-diagonal flag (skips reading diagonal).
- \`ldl_factor(a) → (L, D)\` — no-pivot LDL^T decomposition. Returns unit lower-triangular \`L\` (n × n) and diagonal \`D\` (length-n vector) such that \`A = L diag(D) L^T\`. Reliable on PD / strongly diagonally-dominant inputs.
- \`ldl_solve(l, d, b) → x\` — three-step solve: \`L y = b\` (forward, unit diag), \`D z = y\` (scalar division), \`L^T x = z\` (back, unit diag, transpose).
- \`householder_product(v, tau) → Q\` — applies the implicit Householder representation from a QR factorization. Returns orthogonal \`Q\` of shape \`[m, m]\`. Handles k=0 (returns identity).
- \`matrix_exp(a) → expm(a)\` — Padé(13) approximation with scaling-and-squaring. Higham 2005 thresholds (θ_13 = 5.37...). Pure-Rust f64 LU-with-partial-pivoting solves the (V - U)·X = (V + U) inner system.
- \`cholesky_ex\` / \`inv_ex\` / \`solve_ex\` — non-throwing variants. Each returns \`(value, info)\` where \`info\` is a 0-d scalar tensor: \`0\` on success, \`1\` on failure (with a zero-filled tensor of the right shape).

### Tests (18 new in \`linalg::tests\`)
- \`solve_triangular\`: lower / upper / 2-D RHS / unit-diagonal / transpose
- \`ldl_factor\` reconstruction (\`A = L diag(D) L^T\`)
- \`ldl_solve\` matches \`solve\` on PD input
- \`matrix_exp\`: zero → I, diagonal → diag(exp), 2x2 skew-symmetric → rotation matrix
- \`cholesky_ex\` / \`inv_ex\` / \`solve_ex\`: success path returns info=0; failure path returns info≠0 without panicking
- \`householder_product\`: identity for k=0, single-reflection orthogonality

## Verification
- \`cargo test -p ferrotorch-core linalg::tests\`: 84 pass (18 new + 66 existing)
- \`cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast --features cuda\`: zero regressions

## Notes & follow-ups
- LDL is no-pivot. For symmetric indefinite or rank-deficient matrices, Bunch-Kaufman is the right choice; left as future work since faer exposes it but plumbing the StackReq / MemBuffer machinery is non-trivial. \`ldl_factor\` errors with "zero pivot" if the path encounters one — caller can fall back to \`eigh\`.
- All ops CPU-only today; matches the existing linalg surface (\`det\`, \`pinv\`, \`lstsq\`, \`lu\`, etc are also CPU). GPU dispatch is out of scope here and would belong with the cuSOLVER refactor in #604.
